prerequisites: A running Starburst Galaxy cluster, with its server hostname, username, and password available from the cluster's Connection info pane.
An Amazon S3 catalog created in Starburst Galaxy and attached to the running cluster, referenced by the catalog config field.
An AWS S3 bucket for staging data, plus an AWS access key ID and secret access key that can read from and write to it.
The Galaxy user's role granted the privileges needed on the target S3 catalog and bucket location.
Acceptance of the Starburst Galaxy terms and conditions (accept_terms must be set to true).

authMethods.name: Galaxy user (username and password)
authMethods.summary: Connect to the Galaxy cluster with the username and password config fields. The username is the Galaxy user shown in the cluster Connection info; for client connections the role can be appended to the username as username/role (some clients require the / to be URL-encoded as %2F). Connections use TLS on port 443 by default.

authMethods.name: AWS S3 access key
authMethods.summary: Authenticate to the S3 staging bucket using the staging_object_store.s3_access_key_id and staging_object_store.s3_secret_access_key fields. These map to the AWS access key and secret key that Starburst Galaxy supports for S3 object storage access.

setupSteps.title: Collect Galaxy cluster connection info
setupSteps.body: In Starburst Galaxy, open your running cluster and go to its Connection info pane. Note the server hostname, the Galaxy username, and the password. Use these for the server_hostname, username, and password fields. Leave port at its default of 443 unless your cluster uses a different port.

setupSteps.title: Create and attach an S3 catalog
setupSteps.body: Create an Amazon S3 catalog in Starburst Galaxy and attach it to the running cluster. Set the catalog field to this catalog's name. Optionally set catalog_schema to the default schema to write into; if omitted it defaults to public.

setupSteps.title: Configure the S3 staging object store
setupSteps.body: Provide the staging_object_store settings for the S3 bucket used to stage data: bucket name, bucket path, and bucket region. Set staging_object_store.s3_access_key_id and staging_object_store.s3_secret_access_key to an AWS access key and secret key with read/write access to that bucket.

setupSteps.title: Accept terms and set sync options
setupSteps.body: Set accept_terms to true to agree to the Starburst Galaxy terms and conditions. Optionally adjust purge_staging_table, which controls whether the intermediate staging table is removed after each sync (enabled by default).
